questions.question: What structural pattern does a Hard Link follow compared to a Symbolic Link?
answers: Hard links delete the original file target when broken; symbolic links do not
A Hard Link points directly to the same inode; a Symbolic Link points to a path string
Hard links require administrative privileges to execute
Symbolic links can only reference binary files; hard links handle folders

questions.question: What does an inode store in a Unix-like file system?
answers: The encryption keys used for secure network transmissions
File metadata, size, permissions, and block pointers, but not the file name
The human-readable file name and directory path string
The literal plain-text contents of the target file

questions.question: What is the primary role of the Virtual File System (VFS) layer in a kernel?
answers: To manage virtual memory swap spaces on physical storage disks
To buffer incoming network package flows before delivery to sockets
To provide an abstract uniform interface for applications to access diverse file systems
To simulate internal hard drive arrays inside local ram spaces

questions.question: What differentiates a process from a thread?
answers: A single thread can contain multiple independent processes
A process has its own isolated address space; threads share address spaces
Processes share file descriptors by default; threads do not
Threads run in kernel mode; processes run in user mode

questions.question: Which scheduling algorithm runs processes based on a fixed time-slice quantum rotation?
answers: Shortest Job First (SJF)
First-Come, First-Served (FCFS)
Round Robin (RR)
Multilevel Feedback Queue

questions.question: What is a major disadvantage of the Shortest Job First scheduling algorithm?
answers: It requires specialized graphical hardware layers
Massive memory cache utilization footprints
Long-running processes can suffer from starvation
High context-switching processing overhead

questions.question: What information is tracked inside a Process Control Block (PCB)?
answers: Local database storage tables metadata
The user web browser cache profiles
Network interface card MAC address parameters
Process state, program counter, CPU registers, and memory boundaries

questions.question: What happens during a hardware interrupt?
answers: The operating system completely reboots its kernel
The active process is terminated and marked as a zombie
Virtual memory maps flush physical hard drive tracks
The CPU suspends its execution stream to run an interrupt service routine

questions.question: What characterizes a monolithic kernel architecture?
answers: It eliminates context switching overhead entirely
OS services are moved to separate user-space modules
All operating system services run inside a single privileged kernel space
It runs exclusively on virtual machines

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a system call?
answers: To clear CPU cache configurations automatically
To manage graphical window rendering pipelines
To compile source code files into machine code
To provide an interface for user programs to request services from the kernel
